features.name: Console Rendering
features.description: High-level Console class that renders rich text, tables, and panels to the terminal with automatic width detection.

features.name: Syntax Highlighting
features.description: Pygments-based code syntax highlighting with customizable themes and line numbers.

features.name: Progress Bars
features.description: Multi-task progress bar system with customizable columns, transfer speed, and ETA calculations.

features.name: Table Rendering
features.description: Rich table rendering with automatic column sizing, borders, row styling, and cell alignment.

features.name: Markdown Rendering
features.description: Terminal-native markdown rendering with headings, code blocks, links, and inline formatting.

features.name: Rich Tracebacks
features.description: Enhanced Python traceback rendering with syntax highlighting, local variables, and frame context.

features.name: Live Display
features.description: Real-time updating display for dynamic content like spinners, status indicators, and live logs.

call_chain.role: Primary output method that accepts any renderable, converts it to segments, and writes to the terminal.
Converts renderables into a flat list of Segment objects by calling their __rich_console__ protocol.
Atomic rendering unit containing a text string, optional style, and control flag.
Protocol implementation that yields styled Segments for each span of formatted text.
Converts a Style into ANSI escape sequences for terminal color and formatting output.

console_rendering.explanation: Rich's console rendering follows a protocol-based architecture. When Console.print() is called, it passes each argument through Console.render() which calls the __rich_console__ method on each renderable object. This yields Segment objects — the atomic unit of Rich's rendering pipeline, each containing text and a Style. The Style is then converted to ANSI escape sequences for terminal output. The Console handles width detection, word wrapping, and output buffering. The protocol design means any object can become renderable by implementing __rich_console__.

call_chain.role: Initializes the progress display with configurable columns, refresh rate, and console binding.
Creates a new tracked task and returns a TaskID for updating progress.
Updates a task's completed count and triggers a re-render of the progress display.
Builds a Table renderable from all active tasks and their progress columns.
Redraws the live display area by diffing the new output against the previous render.

progress_bars.explanation: Rich's progress system combines the Live display with a columnar task table. Progress.__init__() sets up configurable columns (spinner, bar, percentage, ETA, etc.) and creates a Live context. add_task() registers tracked tasks with a total count. As update() is called with completed counts, the progress recalculates percentages, speeds, and ETAs. make_tasks_table() builds a Rich Table with one row per task, where each column renders its own progress widget. The Live display refreshes at a configurable rate, using terminal diff to minimize output.
